summary: Implemented the remaining git worktree lifecycle for kanban workers: completion finalizes worktree output by committing changes on the per-task branch and removing the worktree, failure/crash paths preserve worktrees, and dispatcher ticks prune orphaned completed/archived worktrees.
implementation_notes: resolve_workspace already creates per-task worktrees under <repo>/.worktrees/task_<task_id> with branch kanban/task_<task_id>.

complete_task now calls _finalize_completed_worktree after DB completion, committing dirty worktree contents with message 'kanban: complete <task_id>' and removing the worktree via git worktree remove.
detect_crashed_workers/enforce_max_runtime/reclaim paths do not call finalization, preserving failure worktrees for debugging.
dispatch_once now invokes prune_orphaned_worktrees to remove stale worktrees for done/archived worktree tasks only. Dirty or invalid worktrees are preserved if git refuses removal.
known_scope_notes: Repository had pre-existing unrelated .ai artifact changes and branch state before this run; code changes in this run were limited to kanban_db.py and test_kanban_db.py plus this implementation summary artifact.
